papers.title: The Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI): A Decade of Progress
papers.institution: PPMI / Michael J. Fox Foundation (50 sites worldwide)
papers.access_route: ppmi-info.org data/sample access; Coriell biorepository for specimens
papers.notes: 4200+ participants at 50 sites with serum, PBMC, DNA, CSF, and urine. Full trio (serum+PBMC+DNA) confirmed. Age distribution spans broad PD range — age >75 subset needs filtering from the full cohort; not a dedicated elderly-onset PD collection. Specimens distributed via Coriell Institute. Well-documented access process through PPMI portal.

papers.title: The Parkinson's Disease Biomarkers Program (PDBP): A Resource for PD Research
papers.access_route: NINDS PDBP data access; contact PDBP-OPS@mail.nih.gov; specimens via Coriell
papers.notes: 1082 participants with CSF, DNA, PBMC, and serum. Full trio confirmed. NINDS-funded with formal access via Coriell Institute. Direct operations contact: PDBP-OPS@mail.nih.gov. Age >75 subset available but requires cohort filtering — not pre-stratified by age. Established access infrastructure with NIH backing.

papers.title: Parkinson's Disease within the UK Biobank: Genetic and Phenotypic Characteristics
papers.notes: PD cases within 500K participant UK Biobank. Serum and DNA available; PBMC not confirmed as banked specimen type for UK Biobank. Age >75 cases available given UK Biobank age range (40-69 at recruitment, now older with follow-up). PBMC gap is the key limitation for this bundle. Formal application-based access with processing fees.

expected_retrievable_institutions: PPMI / Michael J. Fox Foundation (via Coriell)
notes: Strong sourcing landscape. PPMI and PDBP both have the full serum+PBMC+DNA trio with formal access via Coriell Institute. The age >75 constraint is the key filter: neither PPMI nor PDBP pre-stratify by age >75, but both are large enough (1082–4200 participants) to yield sufficient elderly-onset PD cases. UK Biobank has serum+DNA but PBMC availability is unconfirmed — classify as partial. Eval should check: (1) what fraction of PPMI/PDBP n falls in the >75 age bucket, and (2) whether UK Biobank has PBMC or equivalent stored PBMCs for the PD sub-cohort.
